title: Handle Tableau GraphQL 20K Record Limit with On-Demand Loading
description:
Refactor Tableau metadata queries to handle the GraphQL API 20,000 record limit.

### Problem
Previously, all workbooks, dashboards, sheets, and fields were fetched in a single GraphQL query. For larger datasets, this exceeded Tableau's 20K record limit.

### Solution
- Fetch workbooks and dashboards together in the initial query
- Load sheets and sheet fields on-demand when a user clicks into a specific dashboard
- Tableau's GraphQL API does not support pagination for top-level connections (`workbooks`, `dashboards`); pagination only works for deeper connections like `datasourceConnections`

### Constraints
- If a customer has >20K workbooks + dashboards combined, they hit the limit on initial load
- If sheet-field combinations for a single dashboard exceed 20K, that also triggers the limit
- These are inherent Tableau GraphQL API limitations
acceptance_criteria: Initial load fetches workbooks and dashboards without exceeding 20K limit for typical use cases
Sheet and field data loads on-demand when clicking a dashboard
middleware_changes: Refactored Tableau GraphQL queries to split workbook/dashboard fetch from sheet/field fetch. On-demand loading of sheet-level data.
api_changes: Split single monolithic GraphQL query into two: (1) workbooks + dashboards, (2) sheets + fields per dashboard.
implementation_notes: Tableau GraphQL API has a hard 20K record limit per query. Top-level connections do not support pagination. Solution splits queries and defers sheet/field loading to click-time.
comment_summary: Developer detailed the refactoring: initial query fetches workbooks and dashboards together, sheet/field data is loaded on-demand per dashboard. Noted Tableau's lack of top-level pagination as a fundamental limitation.
